buzzr 1.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Handling time data in buzzr

buzzr: Tools for wrangling buzzdetect results

Source: R/buzzr-package.R
buzzr-package.Rd

buzzr provides a pipeline for turning raw buzzdetect results into plotable, modelable, useful results.

Do it the easy way

Run bin_directory to automate all of the steps below.

Step-by-step workflow

A step-by-step workflow can be nice if you're still working out your pipeline. For example, maybe you want to tune your threshold, so you don't want bin_directory to re-read all of the results every time. First read_directory, then try out a bunch of different thresholds to call_detections.

1. Read results

Point read_directory at the output folder from buzzdetect. Optionally, let buzzr convert file times to real-world date times by supplying a POSIX format string (e.g. '%y%m%d_%H%M') and time zone. Tell buzzr how to understand your data organization by turning path levels (e.g. ./year/site/recorder/foo_buzzdetect.csv) into columns.

2. Call detections

Pass the raw activation values through call_detections with a named threshold vector (e.g. c(ins_buzz = -1.2)). This produces a detections_ column for each named neuron, where the value is TRUE if its activation exceeds your threshold for that frame.

3. Bin by time

Bin frame-level detections into fixed-width time bins with bin Set calculate_rate = TRUE to add a detectionrate_ column (detections / frames).

4. Plot

Use commontime to collapse recordings from different dates onto a shared time-of-day axis, label_hour to format the x-axis, and theme_buzzr for a clean ggplot2 theme.

For a complete worked example see vignette('buzzr').

Key functions

bin_directory

One-stop-shop. Read, threshold, and bin an entire results folder in one call.

read_results

Read a single buzzdetect CSV or RDS result file.

call_detections

Apply activation thresholds to produce binary detections.

bin

Aggregate frame-level results into time bins.

commontime

Collapse dates onto a shared time-of-day axis for plotting.

theme_buzzr

A ggplot2 theme styled for buzzdetect result plots.

Useful tools

trim_directory

Dataset too big? Drop unnecessary detail and save as compressed RDS objects (faster to read, too!)

See also

Useful links:

  • https://OSU-Bee-Lab.github.io/buzzr

  • https://github.com/OSU-Bee-Lab/buzzdetect
